commit.message:
fix(auth): use signInWithOtp for OTP resend to trigger Send Email Hook (#1001)

* fix(tests): handle prebuild-generated gradle.properties in SDK 55 test

expo prebuild adds newArchEnabled to android/gradle.properties, but
this file is gitignored. Skip the check when the file doesn't exist
and filter out prebuild-injected lines when it does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-storefront): cast ctaLink to satisfy Expo Router typed routes

The HeroSlide ctaLink type expects Expo Router's route union, but
the slides come from dynamic config data as plain strings. Cast
to the expected type since the links are validated at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add max_age=0 to force fresh OAuth session on retry

Jumia's /login endpoint detects prior sessions and redirects to
/connect/auth/callback with a stale session id, returning "no route
matched". Adding max_age=0 (OpenID Connect) forces Jumia to ignore
cached sessions and always present a fresh login page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): use signInWithOtp for OTP resend to trigger Send Email Hook

supabase.auth.resend() bypasses the custom Send Email Hook (send-auth-email
edge function → ZeptoMail), causing resend emails to silently fail since no
Supabase SMTP is configured. Replace with signInWithOtp() which properly
triggers the hook. Both verify screens already handle the OTP type fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): address CodeRabbit review feedback

- Strengthen timer-blocked test: assert button exists and is disabled
- Use @/ path alias for test import
- Add navigation assertion after successful OTP verification
- Document OTP type:'email' vs type:'signup' fallback in comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): address CodeRabbit round-2 review feedback

- Use Supabase { error } response shape in mocks instead of rejections
- Swap verify fallback order: try type:'email' first (matches signInWithOtp)
- Type RHF useController mock strictly, remove any casts
- Use aria-label + getByRole for OTP input (accessibility best practice)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: target tmux sessions by id

Prefer session ids over session names when issuing tmux commands from Session helpers so overlapping names do not resolve via tmux prefix matching.

This updates session-scoped operations to use an id-first target helper with a fallback to Name for manually constructed Session values that do not have an Id. It also refreshes Session.Name after Rename succeeds so follow-on operations do not depend on stale local state.

The change extends coverage with unit tests for id-first and fallback behavior plus an integration test that reproduces the reported claude/claude2 collision and verifies only the intended session is removed.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Hash plain finite-field vectors and matrices (#78)

Add a generic finite-field vector hash helper that computes the
minimal field of the entries via BaseDomain and feeds
NumberFFVector. Reuse it for non-compressed matrices by hashing
rows with the existing matrix-list combiner while leaving
compressed GF(2) and 8bit methods alone.

Resolves #77

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Commit trait for Result<T, E> (#45)

## Summary

- Adds a blanket `impl<T: Commit, E: core::fmt::Debug> Commit for
Result<T, E>` in `airbender-core/src/guest.rs`
- `Ok(val)` delegates to `val.commit_words()`
- `Err(e)` panics with `{:?}` formatting of the error — consistent with
the guest environment's expectation that only successful results are
committed

## Test plan

- [x] `commit_words_result_ok` — verifies `Ok(42u32)` produces the same
words as `42u32.commit_words()`
- [x] `commit_words_result_err_panics` — verifies `Err(...)` panics with
the expected message
- [x] All 13 existing tests continue to pass (`cargo test -p
airbender-core`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: add mypy config and enable check (#896)

- Add [tool.mypy] to pyproject.toml — ported from roboflow/supervision (strict=true, ignore_missing_imports=false, python_version="3.10", mypy_path="src"); add per-module ignore_missing_imports overrides for torchvision/pycocotools/timm/einops; add ignore_errors overrides for 54 pre-existing failing modules
- Add mirrors-mypy v1.19.1 hook to .pre-commit-config.yaml (after ruff, with --ignore-missing-imports for isolated env)
- Add .github/workflows/ci-typing.yml — runs mypy src/rfdetr/ on push/PR with CPU-only torch and full strict config from pyproject.toml
- Split typing dependencies into a dedicated group in `pyproject.toml`.
- Update `ci-typing.yml` to install only the typing group for type-checking.
- Remove `--ignore-missing-imports` from the pre-commit mypy hook configuration.

* Fix mypy typing issues across utility, dataset, inference, and training modules

commit.message:
docs: highlight main and support crates in API docs (#44)

## Summary

Adds clear "main" vs "support" crate distinction across the API docs
landing page and individual crate documentation.

**API docs index page (`scripts/`)**
- `generate_api_index.py`: introduces a `MAIN_CRATES` set
(`airbender-sdk`, `airbender-host`, `cargo-airbender`) and emits
per-card role badges and CSS classes; the template now renders two
separate grids via `{{ main_crate_cards }}` / `{{ support_crate_cards
}}`.
- `api-index.html.template`: splits the crate grid into two labelled
sections — *Main crates* (highlighted with accent border and orange
badge) and *Support crates* (subdued badge). Section descriptions guide
users to the right starting point.

**Support crate READMEs** (shown verbatim in rustdoc via `#![doc =
include_str!("../README.md")]`)

Each of the seven support crates gains a `> [!NOTE]` callout immediately
after the alpha warning, naming the main crate that re-exports its API:
- `airbender-rt` → `airbender-sdk`
- `airbender-guest` → `airbender-sdk`
- `airbender-macros` → `airbender-sdk`
- `airbender-codec` → `airbender-sdk` / `airbender-host`
- `airbender-core` → `airbender-sdk` / `airbender-host`
- `airbender-crypto` → `airbender-sdk` (crypto feature)
- `airbender-build` → `cargo-airbender`

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `python3 scripts/generate_api_index.py --workspace-root .
--template scripts/templates/api-index.html.template --output
/tmp/api-index.html` and verify the output contains two grids with
correct badges.
- [ ] Check that all seven support crate READMEs render the NOTE callout
on GitHub.
- [ ] Verify `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` succeeds and the
generated rustdoc pages include the NOTE callouts (since READMEs are
embedded via `include_str!`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

feat(cli): ship Windows and GNU release targets (#16)

* feat(cli): ship Windows and GNU release targets

Summary:
- add Windows npm targets and GNU Linux release extras to the CLI
  packaging metadata and launcher fallback logic
- build real Windows self-host helper binaries in GitHub Actions and
  smoke test the packaged Windows tarballs on hosted runners
- enable packaged self-host runtime support on Windows with .exe
  resolution, shared process liveness checks, and stop markers

Rationale:
- the release pipeline only shipped the bundled self-host runtime for
  Unix targets, which left Windows builds without a usable helper
- we do not maintain a dedicated Windows builder, so GitHub Actions
  must produce and validate the Windows helper artifacts

Tests:
- turbo run check:fix
- bun run format
- bun run typecheck
- bun run lint --format json
- bun run test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(repo): split server integration into manual workflow

Summary:
- remove the slow server integration lane from the default CI workflow
  and its required results gate
- add a dedicated manual-only server integration workflow that keeps
  the existing Postgres setup and integration test command

Rationale:
- the integration lane is materially slower than the normal PR checks
  and should not run automatically on every change
- moving it to a separate workflow keeps the coverage available when
  maintainers need it without blocking regular CI

Tests:
- actionlint .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/server-integration.yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix Docker Codex bootstrap/runtime behavior (#77)

* feat(setup): clarify status prompts and local bootstrap

Summary:
- add per-prompt help text for interactive Project status mapping
- mirror the same explanations in the manual status mapping fallback
- add a local bootstrap make target and document how to use it

Rationale:
- the status mapping prompts were too terse for first-time setup users
- testing the remote bootstrap flow should not require merging to main first

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test setup::tests::
- make -n docker-bootstrap-local

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(make): proxy local bootstrap target at repo root

Summary:
- add a top-level make target that forwards docker-bootstrap-local
- preserve the rust makefile defaults by letting submake inherit command-line vars

Rationale:
- users naturally run the bootstrap test command from the repo root
- the root-level proxy removes an avoidable make target mismatch

Tests:
- make -n docker-bootstrap-local RECONFIGURE=1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(docker): stabilize Codex bootstrap runtime

Summary:
- separate the bootstrap repo source from the managed workspace clone URL
- add local bootstrap helpers, setup guidance, and safer project-status copy
- harden Docker Codex runs with runtime caches, home-scoped skills, and quieter logs
- make workspace/bootstrap scripts preserve a usable default-branch baseline

Rationale:
- local bootstrap tests were cloning and operating on the wrong repository
- Docker workers were failing on missing tools, cache permissions, and noisy
  runtime errors instead of cleanly surfacing repo-level failures
- Codex needed stronger path and script guidance to avoid avoidable shell
  mistakes in unknown repositories

Tests:
- cargo fmt --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml config::tests::default_prompt_includes_repo_layout_guidance
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::filters_codex_telemetry_noise
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::downstream_build_diagnostics_are_debug_only
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::detects_error_like_stderr_lines
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::mod::tests::codex_repo_support_dirs_only_require_repo_owned_files
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml workspace::tests::hook_commands_get_runtime_tool_cache_env_defaults
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml workspace::tests::docker_workspace_scripts_exclude_seeded_support_dirs_from_git_status
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml orchestrator::tests::downgrades_expected_probe_failures
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml setup::tests::workflow_template_uses_env_placeholders
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml setup::tests::docker_workflow_describes_repo_owned_workspace_behavior
- local Docker rebuild and soak check via docker compose on kairastra-local-bootstrap

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): address clippy and bootstrap review follow-ups

Summary:
- replace the clippy-blocked iterator loop and async test env locking
- clear stale home-scoped Codex skills when the seed repo removes them
- switch bootstrap seed sync to rsync permission flags instead of recursive chmod
- narrow default-branch baseline fetches to the current and default branches

Rationale:
- PR #77 was failing rust-checks on clippy warnings introduced by the runtime
  and workspace test changes
- review comments flagged stale persisted skills and expensive seed volume
  permission walks in the Docker bootstrap path
- baseline setup only needs the active branch pair, not every remote branch

Tests:
- cargo fmt --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml --check
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml config::tests::default_prompt_includes_repo_layout_guidance
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::detects_error_like_stderr_lines
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml setup::tests::workflow_template_uses_env_placeholders
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml workspace::tests::docker_workspace_scripts_exclude_seeded_support_dirs_from_git_status
- cargo clippy --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(docker): migrate legacy workspace codex support

* fix(docker): remove legacy repo codex state

* fix(docker): chown overridden cache paths

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix npm entrypoints for init and create

Restore the published npm entrypoint contract for @fedify/init and
@fedify/create by making tsdown emit .js and .d.ts outputs again.
This aligns the generated dist files with the existing package.json
bin, main, exports, and types fields.

Add Node regression tests that verify the built entrypoints exist and
that the CLI startup path succeeds with --help, covering the failure
reported in the issue.

Update CHANGES.md with the packaging fix details for the affected
packages.

AI-assisted with OpenAI Codex: prepared the build config changes,
regression tests, changelog update, and verification.

Fixes https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/issues/655

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Eject a possibly stale workspace cache so future mutations don't rely on it.

With `but commit` we noticed raciness, which is now handled by not only
getting a write-lock early on commit, but also by ensuring the workspace cache
is not stale after `init_ctx()` read it *before* changing the workspace materially.
